task:
[Read from: /home/spencer/Repositories/predictability-aware-training/plan.md, /home/spencer/Repositories/predictability-aware-training/progress.md]

RED-TEAM ROUND 2 — strategy, positioning, and next steps of /home/spencer/Repositories/predictability-aware-training. Read RESEARCH.md (especially the re-scoped novelty claim + training-time prior art table), RESULTS.md (all 6 experiments + red-team sections), PLAN.md. The project plans Tier C next: fine-tune OLMoE-1B-7B (real pretrained MoE, Colibri-supported) with the joint predictability loss. Attack the strategy: (1) Is Tier C the right next move, or is something cheaper/more decisive being skipped? Consider: the unresolved StickyMoE baseline (lambda_sticky=0.1 was 'too weak', inconclusive, and left hanging), the missing ranking-aware SOTA posthoc control (2511.10676 protocol), a longer Tier A run (the 75x-undertrained confound hangs over EVERY claim — would 10x more tokens change the answer?), or TTFT measurement. Rank what you would actually do next and why. (2) Tier C design critique: OLMoE fine-tune with router+predictor-only trainable vs full fine-tune — what does each prove? Is 1B-active/7B-total with 64 experts the right target? What does success/failure look like, and can this box (31 TFLOPS bf16 iGPU, 128GB) execute it in reasonable time? (3) Publication posture: given StickyMoE/Oracle-MoE/ReMoE/Pre-gated MoE, what is the strongest honest framing — a workshop paper? a systems co-design paper? What experiments would a skeptical MLSys/ICLR reviewer demand that are missing? (4) The throughput reframing in Exp 5 finding 5 ('accuracy multiplier on the prefetch component', 'TTFT may be the headline') — is this a graceful retreat or the truth? What measurement would settle it? (5) Anything in the git history/docs that overclaims? Check RESEARCH.md/RESULTS.md/PLAN.md wording against the actual evidence. READ-ONLY. Ranked findings with severity and concrete actions.

criteria.must: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable
Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: 8 ranked findings with file paths and severities (e.g., High: RESULTS.md Exp 4 finding 5 sticky baseline unmeasured; High: PLAN.md Tier C frozen-backbone design flaw; Medium: README.md 'quality is free' contradicted by Exp 6 +0.023+-0.009 nats), plus answers to all 5 red-team questions grounded in RESEARCH.md/RESULTS.md/PLAN.md/model.py/cache_sim.py and git log

commandsRun.summary: 10 commits, messages verified against evidence; only untracked .pi-subagents artifacts

commandsRun.command: grep -n sticky train.py model.py; grep tok/s run_seeds.log run_tierA2.log; grep queue/demand cache_sim.py
commandsRun.summary: StickyMoE loss implemented (model.py:86-89); Tier A ~35.7k tok/s (12 min/25M tokens); cache_sim FIFO demand-priority queue verified honest

validationOutput: READ-ONLY review: no files changed, no tests to run
Verified: Exp 6 stats, Exp 4 isolation test, cache_sim economics, Exp 5 headroom math (25% of base-to-oracle), git history matches evidence
Verified overclaims: README.md quality-free headline vs Exp 6; PLAN.md Tier C 'hours' and 450M-vs-341M drift
residualRisks: External prior-art and Colibri claims taken as cited, not independently verified
If a tuned StickyMoE baseline matches joint-training hit@k, the re-scoped novelty claim collapses — this is the highest-priority open experiment
Tier C as currently designed (backbone frozen) would produce a ReMoE-adjacent result that does not test the core backbone-predictability mechanism
Pretrained OLMoE router malleability at small fine-tune token budgets is unknown
childReport.diffSummary: No diff — read-only red-team review of strategy, Tier C design, publication posture, TTFT reframing, and doc overclaims
reviewFindings: high: RESULTS.md Exp 4 finding 5 — StickyMoE baseline left inconclusive (single weak lambda, no hit@k) despite RESEARCH.md calling it the mandatory baseline; run lambda sweep + posthoc-on-sticky + stacking before Tier C

high: PLAN.md Tier C — router+predictor-only with frozen backbone cannot test the backbone-predictability mechanism (Exp 4 finding 1) and collapses into ReMoE territory; unfreeze last-k blocks or LoRA, and define success/failure criteria
medium: RESULTS.md red-team #5 — posthoc control undermatched vs arXiv 2511.10676 (2-layer ranking-aware head); +6.2-6.8pt effect size not yet defended
medium: 75-80x undertrained confound (25M tokens) hangs over all claims; a 250M-token run costs ~1.5h/arm at 35.7k tok/s and should precede Tier C
medium: RESULTS.md Exp 5 finding 5 — 'TTFT may be the headline' is unmeasured; add cold-cache TTFT/percentile latency to cache_sim.py before claiming it
medium: README.md — 'Quality is free up to lambda ~= 0.3' contradicted by Exp 6 (+0.023+-0.009 nats, 'NOT right at Tier A'); Status section stale (Tier A complete)
low: PLAN.md — Tier C 'hours' wall time only valid at <=25M tokens; full FT ~110GB optimizer+weights exceeds budget; Tier A 450M (PLAN/model.py:34) vs 341M (RESULTS) drift
no blockers to continuing experiments, but Tier C should be sequenced behind the four cheaper decisive items ranked in the review
childReport.manualNotes: Recommended execution order: (1) sticky baseline sweep+eval+stacking, (2) ranking-aware SOTA control, (3) 10x longer Tier A run, (4) cache_sim TTFT measurement, (5) Tier C with an unfreezing strategy. Items 1-4 total ~2-3 days of compute on this box and each can independently kill or strengthen the paper's central claims; Tier C as designed cannot.
